title: China Is Spending Big to Rival India's Buddhist Influence in Asia
summary: As the Dalai Lama succession approaches, China is leveraging Buddhism to expand its influence across Asia, testing whether money and power can rival India’s role as the faith’s birthplace.

title: Stocks Bounce After Tech-Led Selloff, Dollar Falls: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks rose around the world as worries that a rally in the artificial-intelligence trade had run ahead of itself subsided. The dollar was on track for its worst week since April.

title: France’s Lescure Wants 2027 Budget That Brings Deficit Below 5%
summary: French Finance Minister Roland Lescure said he will push for a finance bill for next year that brings a further improvement in public finances, and expects parliament “most likely” will allow a budget to pass.

title: Yen Isn't Behaving Like a G10 Currency, Mizuho Says
summary: Jordan Rochester, Mizuho FICC strategy EMEA head, discusses sharp moves in the yen. "From all the frameworks, dollar-yen should be lower," he tells Bloomberg Television. He adds there has been a change in behaviors in the Japanese space and the yen is not behaving like a G10 currency. "It trades like an EM in terms of its correlation to rates," Rochester says.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Citi Says Oil May Slump to $60 as Hormuz Shock Fades Away
summary: Brent oil could extend declines to $60 a barrel by year-end as disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz ease, according to Citigroup Inc., adding to a chorus of bearish outlooks for the global crude market.

title: South Korean Stocks Jump 6% After Turbulent Week on AI Swings
summary: South Korean stocks rebounded after a near 10% drop in the previous two sessions, highlighting skittish investor sentiment as scrutiny grows over the sustainability of the global AI boom.

title: TotalEnergies Offers Iraqi Oil to Asian Buyers as Supply Swells
summary: French oil major TotalEnergies SE is offering millions of barrels of Iraqi crude that can be promptly delivered to Asia, according to traders, adding to a market already brimming with supply.

title: Renk Group Nears Deal to Buy UK’s David Brown Defence
summary: Industrial machinery maker Renk Group AG has agreed a deal to acquire privately held David Brown Defence, in a move that gives it more exposure to the naval sector.

title: Yen Strengthens as Traders Brace for Holiday Intervention Risk
summary: Options traders are paying up to hedge against sharp moves in the yen ahead of thin US holiday trading, as speculation grows that Japanese authorities may be less predictable in how they intervene to support the currency.

title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 7/3/2026
summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Rises After Selloff With Flows Through Hormuz in Focus
summary: Oil rebounded in thin trading following a sharp selloff, with traders weighing the outlook of increased supply through the Strait of Hormuz and continuing talks between the US and Iran.

title: AI Boom Cements HK's Role as Gateway to China | The China Show | 7/3/2026
summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and David Ingles give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Rises Toward $4,200 as Weak Jobs Data Lowers Rate-Hike Odds
summary: Gold advanced for a third day after weak US jobs data reduced the chances of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates this year to tackle inflation.

title: Traders Weigh Second Half Outlook: Markets Snapshot
summary:
The first half of 2026 saw a bumper earnings season and global equity benchmarks soar to new highs, driven by a enthusiasm around the AI trade. Businesses, investors and central banks now turn their attention to the second half of the year with the focus firmly on whether markets can move past rocky geopolitical issues, growth concerns and ongoing jitters around the AI buildout. The Opening Trade spoke to leading voices about how the rest of 2026 is shaping up for equity markets.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Factories Create Winners and Losers in Power Equipment Market
summary: Next-generation AI factories are forcing power equipment firms to rethink their portfolios in the race to profit from a market expected to be worth more than $200 billion a year.

title: Has the AI Rally Gone Too Far, Too Fast?
summary: Fiona Yang, Invesco Fund Manager for Asia ex-Japan Equities, says the AI trade isn't over but parts of the market have become stretched. She discussed the market dynamics around the tech trade with Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin and Avril Hong on Insight. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The AI Trade Is Losing One of Its Key Signals
summary: At a time when markets are growing uneasy over whether the enormous sums being poured into artificial intelligence will ever pay off, the prices the sector commands for each unit of usage are drifting lower.

title: Korea Said to Prepare for Currency Flow From SK Hynix US Listing
summary: South Korean officials are preparing for currency flows related to SK Hynix Inc.’s offering of American depositary receipts as soon as Friday, according to a person familiar with the matter.

title: Carlsberg Confidentially Files $700 Million India IPO
summary: Carlsberg A/S confidentially filed draft papers for an initial public offering of its India unit, according to a public advertisement.

title: FTSE 100 Hits Highest Since March as Rally Extends
Choppy AI Trade, Fed Outlook Drive Asia Markets | Insight with Haslinda Amin 07/03/2026
summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: StanChart Targets Mid-Sized African Companies for Debt Sales
summary: Standard Chartered Plc said a $50 million green bond it placed last month for a solar home system financier opens the way for a slew of debt sales by mid-sized African companies.

title: Scarred by ‘97 Crash, Korea Nervously Opens 24-Hour Won Trading
summary: Deep inside a government complex in Sejong, about two hours from Seoul, a room known as “the box” is ground zero for South Korea’s currency anxieties.

title: LVMH Wins Court Case Over Logo Used by China Tea Chain
summary: A Chinese milk tea brand has been ordered to pay $1.5 billion to French luxury giant Louis Vuitton for trademark infringement. The case has sparked widespread discussion on Chinese social media about intellectual property protection. Bloomberg's China correspondent Minmin Low reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ICICI Bank Mulls First Benchmark Dollar Bond Sale Since 2017
summary: ICICI Bank Ltd., India’s second-largest private-sector lender, is in talks to raise at least $500 million in offshore bonds as it seeks to capitalize on a recent Reserve Bank of India concession for overseas borrowings, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Indian State Objects to MSC’s $1.4 Billion Deal With Adani Ports
summary: An Indian state is objecting to Mediterranean Shipping Co.’s planned $1.4 billion investment in an Adani Group-run terminal, intensifying scrutiny of the Swiss firm after a chemical spill last year involving one of its vessels.

title: Europe Now Believes Some Hormuz Fees Will Be Inevitable
summary: Some leading European powers now accept that ships transiting the vital Strait of Hormuz will have to pay fees to Iran and Oman, according to people familiar with the matter. Bloomberg's Patrick Sykes has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: India, Japan Agree to Deepen Economic Ties
summary: India and Japan have agreed to deepen cooperation on energy, technology, and defense during Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s first official visit to New Delhi. Former Indian Ambassador to Japan, Deepa Gopalan Wadhwa, dicusses the implications of the visit on Bloomberg's Insight with Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Race to Rescue 8,000 Sailors Still Stranded Behind Hormuz
summary: After more than 100 days trapped in the Persian Gulf, thousands of seafarers are still waiting for a chance to sail out of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: ASX Bets New CEO Can Restore Confidence After 30% Share Drop
summary: When Anthony Attia takes the helm at Australia’s ASX Ltd., the world’s worst-performing stock exchange operator by share price, he’ll need to launch straight into a battle to win over regulators and investors.

title: Bracing for Yen Swings; US Jobs Ease Fed-Hike Concerns | The Asia Trade 7/3/2026
summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)
